channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Jynxzi Couldn’t Save LCS Spring 2026 From Record Low Viewership
item.content:encoded:
The League of Legends Championship Series (LCS) drew the North American league’s lowest-ever viewership in Spring 2026.

The LCS Spring 2026 regular season attracted a new low of 86k viewers as interest in the LoL league dropped.

Now, according to Esports Charts, the LCS Spring 2026 Playoffs only peaked at 183,152 live viewers, an all-time low for an LCS split.

The peak viewership came during the LCS Playoffs final as LYON swept Team Liquid in three straight games.

If you haven’t already, download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android:

  • Android Link: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.insidergaming.app
  • iOS Link: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/insider-gaming/id6753984648

Jynxzi Fails to Have an Impact

And this was despite the fact that popular streamer Nicholas ‘Jynxzi’ Stewart co-streamed the final between LYON and Team Liquid.

Earlier this year, Jynxzi hosted a content creator LoL tournament with over 900k viewers, one of the most-watched LoL events this year.

In comparison, his co-stream of the LCS finals appears to have peaked at only 44,416.

Though, that comparison may be unfair considering the content creator event benefitted from various streamer’s audiences and the free Twitch Drops.

LCS Spring 2026 Flops

Surprisingly, the LCS Spring Split Playoffs’ peak audience of just over 183k was not NA’s biggest audience in 2026.

The LYON and Team Liquid final just barely failed to beat the LCS Lock-In, which peaked at 184,969 viewers during LYON vs Cloud9.

It seems the continued failures of Cloud9, one of the LCS’s most decorated a popular teams, is hurting the league.

Related News: Fnatic CEO confirms there have been acquisition bids but claims the brand “isn’t going anywhere.”

Before this year, no LCS split had failed to crack the 200,000 viewers milestone.

There was also an alarming drop off in average viewership this split.

LCS Spring averaged 63,949 viewers, while the Lock-In recorded 89,516 during the Lock-In, representing a drop of almost 29%.

What does the LCS need to do to make the league interesting again? Share your thoughts with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

In other LCS-adjacent news, Team Liquid face a stern test as the MSI 2026 Play-In Bracket has been revealed.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Rumors Of Xbox Reversing Exclusive Plans “False”, Says Executive
item.content:encoded:
With all of the rumors, reports, and scuttlebutt surrounding Xbox right now, a new one regarding the company’s plans for exclusives has made the rounds.

Online speculation picked up on Monday that Xbox was already set to reverse course on its plans for exclusive games. There had even been claims that it was only said and done as a PR stunt. But one Xbox executive has already gone on record to discredit those rumors.

Before going any further, download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Xbox Moving Forward With Exclusives

In a reply to Windows Central’s Jez Corden on Twitter, Xbox Chief Strategy Officer Matthew Ball called the rumors “false”. He said that in addition to the already confirmed upcoming exclusives, there will be more in the years to come.

“These rumors are false,” Ball said. “Gears of War: E-Day and Clockwork Revolution will stay exclusive. There are no conversations and have been no conversations to ‘reverse course.’

“And as we said last week, players can continue to expect signature exclusives from us every year.”

Major Changes Coming to Xbox

As mentioned, there are a lot of reports surrounding Xbox right now. Insider Gaming recently confirmed with internal sources that the company was planning to close South of Midnight developer Compulsion Games. In addition, other studios facing closure include Ninja Theory and Double Fine. I’ve also been told that Arkane Lyon is a studio at risk.

Efforts, however, are being made to keep them open in some way.

What do you think of Xbox’s CSO reaffirming the company’s stance on exclusives moving forward? What about the reported closures of several studios? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, or join the discussion in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about the struggles surrounding the development of the next Ghost Recon game at Ubisoft. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

MOBILE APP

item.title: XBOX Game Pass June 2026 Wave 2 Titles Revealed
item.content:encoded:
XBOX is bringing some major franchises to its Game Pass subscription service to round out June 2026. Among these titles is day-one access to Junkster beginning today.

As the Asha Sharma-led XBOX continues to navigate the troublesome waters of the gaming industry today while trying to reclaim some of its esteem from its glory days, the brand has once again announced a new wave of titles coming to different tiers of its Game Pass subscription service.

Before getting into the meat of the matter, here’s how you can have the cleanest reading experience for this and all of our stories: download the free Insider Gaming App for iOS and Android. 

  • Android Link: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.insidergaming.app
  • iOS Link: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/insider-gaming/id6753984648

The Second Wave of XBOX Game Pass Releases in June 2026 Brings Major Titles

The second wave of games coming to XBOX Games Pass in June 2026 includes major franchises such as Call of Duty and EA Sports FC, along with other titles like Abyssus. The back half of the month brings seven additional titles, with the final two creeping into the first week of July. There’s one game available now via Day One Game Pass. Below, you can check out the complete list of Wave 2 games coming to the platform this month, along with their release dates, platforms, and subscription tiers.

  • Junkster (Cloud, XBOX Series X|S, Handheld, and PC) – June 16: Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass (Day One)
  • Call of Duty: Vanguard (Cloud, XBOX Series X|S, Handheld, and PC) – June 17: Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, and PC Game Pass
  • EA Sports FC 26 (Cloud, XBOX Series X|S, Handheld, and PC) – June 18: Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • Abyssus (Cloud, XBOX Series X|S, and PC) – June 25: Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass
  • RV There Yet? (Cloud, XBOX Series X|S, and PC) – June 30: Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass
  • Tony Hawk’s Pro Skater 3 + 4 (Cloud, Console, and PC) – July 2: Already on Game Pass Premium; coming to Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass
  • Winds of Arcana: Ruination (Cloud, Console, Handheld, and PC) – July 6: Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass

Games Leaving Game Pass on June 30

In addition to the new games coming to the service, several titles are leaving at the end of the month. These games include the iconic Slay the Spire and multiple games in the Tomb Raider franchise. Your last day to pick them up will be June 30. Here’s the full list from the blog:

  • Mecha Break (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Payday 2 (Console)
  • Rise of Tomb Raider (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Tomb Raider (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Slay the Spire (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Ultimate Chicken Horse (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Volcano Princess (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Unpacking (Cloud, Console, and PC)

It’s a tumultuous time for XBOX in the wake of a very strong Showcase this month; news of studio closures continues to hang like a black cloud over any of the positive momentum the brand has gained in recent months. Learn about the closure of Ninja Theory here.

What are your thoughts on the XBOX Game Pass additions for June 2026 Wave 2? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more XBOX from Insider Gaming, read about the company rethinking its approach to next-gen hardware. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP
